created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — woodland group batch; field sheet 10 split per type. v3.3.

ukhab_identity.composite_pk_note: Single Level-4 type; PK = w1e. NB the Crosswalk listed w1e under 'broadleaved' alongside w1a/w1d but mislabelled it — w1e is specifically Upland birchwoods (oak is w1a).
ukhab_identity.annex1_habitat: None specific (birchwoods are not a distinct Annex I type; value is as native woodland and the regeneration frontier)

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (official definitions + master data sheet + applied surveys)
sources.specific_claim: w1e = Upland birchwoods (distinct from w1a Upland oakwood). Confirmed in official UKHab definitions and applied surveys (e.g. SSEN Kintore 'w1e Upland birchwoods').

nvc_communities: W11 / W17 (birch-dominated forms of the upland oak-birch communities)
W4 Betula pubescens–Molinia (wetter birch — overlaps wet woodland)
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: Birch-dominated upland woodland; lower canopy/underwood diversity than oak, but the classic pioneer and regeneration species of the wilder frontier.

ecology.description: Birch-dominated upland woodland — downy birch with rowan, often the pioneer/regeneration phase of the upland wood and the fastest colonist of released open ground. Lower canopy/underwood diversity than oak or ash, but ecologically central as the regeneration frontier of the wilder uplands. Managed by browsing level, not stocked.
condition_indicators_good: Active regeneration and a range of ages; standing/fallen deadwood
Native birch/rowan dominant; a developing understorey and ground flora
Open glades and a graded edge advancing onto former open ground
Browsing light enough that the front keeps advancing
condition_indicators_poor: Overgrazed: an even-aged stand with no young trees; advance halted
Non-native conifer or invasive ingress; deadwood removed
Stalled at a thin even-aged cohort
ecosystem_functions: The pioneer that builds shelter and soil and nurses other tree regeneration
Soil-builder and carbon store; fast early carbon capture
Habitat for birch-associated invertebrates and birds
The leading edge of natural woodland expansion across degraded open ground
ecology.whole_system_role: The regeneration frontier — usually the first woodland to return when browsing is reduced, and the nurse for richer woodland to follow.

successional_neighbours.habitat_code: g1b acid grassland / h1b5 dry heath / scrub (h3)
successional_neighbours.note: Birch is the classic fast colonist of released acid grassland, heath and scrub.
Often a seral phase nursing richer oak or pine woodland over time.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Birch as pioneer/regeneration species; lower diversity than oak; regeneration diagnostic.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; regeneration/advance is the diagnostic.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Multi-aged birch/rowan with active regeneration advancing onto open ground; deadwood present.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Overgrazed, no young trees, advance halted; invasive/conifer ingress.

sources.title: Woodland Condition Assessment
sources.specific_claim: Regeneration and age structure are core indicators.

abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Valley floor to the treeline (birch reaches high)
abiotic_template.aspect_exposure: A wide range; the fast colonist of open slopes
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Throughout the uplands; increases north-west in the oakwoods.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. Bioregionally Wilder (the regeneration frontier); Regenerate where actively colonising degraded ground.

water.state: Free-draining acid ground (wetter forms grade to w1d)
water.descriptor: Mostly free-draining; wet birch grades into wet woodland.

earth.descriptor: Birch litter improves and builds soil quickly — a pioneer soil-builder.

air.exposure_bioclimate: Canopy beginning to buffer the microclimate

fire.descriptor: Fast pioneer growth and early carbon capture; energy flow building toward full woodland.

range_of_natural_variability: New birch colonising released open ground
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: open ground (grassland/heath/scrub) → birch pioneer woodland → (nurses) oak/pine/mixed woodland. The leading edge of woodland return.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: The regeneration frontier — fast pioneer carbon capture and the nurse phase for richer woodland.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: The regeneration frontier — birch returning across degraded open ground (via reduced browsing) is the core Regenerate→Bioregionally Wilder action made visible. Established native birchwood is Bioregionally Wilder; ancient birch stands Protect & Restore. The same 'right tree, right place' exclusions apply (not deep peat, not above the treeline, not over open priority habitats).
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Browsing-controlled. Reduce deer/sheep and birch returns largely on its own where a seed source survives.

wild_management_target.detail: Let birch colonise and advance by reducing browsing; protect the advancing front and the seed source; allow it to nurse richer woodland over time. Keep deadwood.

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: Moderate as a woodland type (lower canopy/underwood diversity than oak or ash), but high STRATEGIC value as the regeneration frontier and soil-building nurse of the wilder uplands.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Widespread and fast to return; not rare, though ancient birch stands have value.

diversity.notes: Lower canopy/underwood diversity; still a full woodland food web developing.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: High pioneer productivity; the leading edge of woodland energy flow returning.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing to let it advance
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: Birch returns largely unaided where browsing is low and a seed source survives (e.g. Ben Lawers); the action is browsing reduction, not stocking.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The advancing pioneer of the wilder mosaic; nurses richer woodland.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: Young birch is palatable browse — heavy grazing halts the advance.
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: The advancing front is the key Bioregionally-Wilder measure.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Birch as the regeneration frontier; browsing reduction brings it back.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Young birch is sought-after browse — which is why the advance halts under heavy grazing. Not a managed forage resource.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: Not a forage/stocking habitat — managed by browsing level for regeneration/advance.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing to allow the front to advance
conservation_lu_ha.scheme: site-specific; not a standard stocking figure
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: At Ben Lawers, reducing grazing brought abundant natural regeneration of pine and birch — browsing reduction is the lever.

sources.title: Woodland Trust / NatureScot regeneration guidance
sources.specific_claim: Reduced browsing brings natural birch/pine regeneration where a seed source survives.

forage.palatability_rank: High (young birch browse) — a regeneration liability

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Let birch advance by lowering deer/sheep.

management.intensity_guidance: Reduce browsing; protect the seed source and advancing front. Planting only where seed source is gone (native, local provenance).
management.regulatory_note: Apply the 'right tree, right place' exclusions (not deep peat, not above the treeline, not over open priority habitats). Control invasives; keep deadwood. Check designated-site consents where relevant.
management.monitoring_protocol: Advancing front/regeneration, age structure, native dominance, transition toward richer woodland, deadwood. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Medium-high distinctiveness as native upland woodland. Strategic value as the regeneration frontier.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Upland birchwood distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

integrity_indicators_fauna: birch-associated invertebrates (a large community) and birds
black grouse on the woodland-moor edge
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full records in Phase 2B. Capture the seral nurse role into w1a/w2a.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (w1e verified; clarified vs w1a oak — the Crosswalk had conflated the broadleaved codes)
the regeneration-frontier framing and browsing-reduction lever
data_completeness.notes: The pioneer/regeneration frontier — usually the first woodland to return when browsing drops, nursing richer oak/pine woodland to follow. Lower canopy diversity than oak/ash but high strategic value. Bioregionally Wilder; the visible leading edge of the Regenerate→Wilder story.

sources.title: Woodland Trust / NatureScot regeneration guidance
sources.specific_claim: Reduced browsing brings natural birch regeneration.
